title: crisp creamy russian beef pelmeni
description: peppery and pillowy show-off russian dish featuring lemongrass stalks, perfect for a special occasion dinner
instructions: in a large mixing bowl, combine beef mince, lemongrass, salt, and black pepper. Mix well with your hands until just combined.
add the flour to the bowl and mix until the dough just comes together. Do not overmix.
turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 5 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
wrap the dough in plastic wrap and let rest for 30 minutes.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and roll out each piece into a thin sheet.
use a round cookie cutter or the rim of a glass to cut out circles of dough. You should be able to get about 20-25 circles.

place a tablespoon of beef filling in the center of each dough circle. Brush the edges with a little water and fold the dough over to form a triangle, pressing the edges to seal. Repeat with the remaining dough and filling.
in a large skillet, melt 20g of butter over medium-high heat. When the butter starts to foam, add a few pelmeni to the pan (do not overcrowd). Cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
remove the pelmeni from the pan and set aside. Repeat with the remaining pelmeni.
add the remaining 30g of butter to the pan and let melt. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
add the cooked pelmeni back to the pan and toss to coat with the onion mixture.
serve the pelmeni hot, topped with a dollop of sour cream and a sprinkle of cheddar cheese (if using). Garnish with chopped fresh dill.
